text:
Heavy waves of airstrikes shook Beirut and Tehran on Wednesday. Three cargo ships came under attack in or near the Strait of Hormuz early in the day; Iran appeared to claim responsibility for one of the strikes. And President Trump told Axios the war would end soon because there was “practically nothing left to target.” But Mr. Trump has also said only Tehran’s “unconditional surrender” would end the war, and Iran has shown no sign of halting its attacks. Here’s how the day unfolded.
Major developments — March 11

SKIP ADVERTISEMENTIran has responded to the U.S.-Israeli assault on the country by launching drones and missiles at American targets across the Middle East, hitting embassies, killing U.S. soldiers, and damaging military bases and air defense infrastructure.
title: This is what happened on March 13.
